mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses the 5.0% threshold, it signals a breaking labor market that reduces consumer spending power and corporate profit growth, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real sales of automobiles and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand across durable goods, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST move together, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-recession recovery phase reflects rapid capacity utilization and margin expansion, generating strong positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative or flat 5-day forward returns during recovery phases, or the predictive power vanishes when GDP growth exceeds 3.0% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and wage income, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth consistently coincides with negative 20-day SPX returns, or the correlation coefficient falls below 0.05 across rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid inflation acceleration forces central banks to tighten policy expectations, raising real discount rates and compressing equity multiples, which generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in > 70% of high-inflation months, or the inflation threshold fails to separate return distributions significantly.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth decelerates while federal funds rates remain elevated, corporate profit margins contract due to higher financing costs and weaker sales, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combination of slowing GDPC1 and high FEDFUNDS fails to predict negative returns, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in panel regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production breaking above its annual trend signals robust manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, which translates into positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: INDPRO crossing above its 12-month average is followed by negative SPX returns in > 60% of cases, or the signal's Sharpe ratio drops below 0.5 in walk-forward tests.
